title: Monotone-constrained strategy booster
summary: Pit urgency should usually increase as tyre-age pressure, service-window overage, and finish infeasibility worsen, while immediately-post-pit freshness should suppress it.
rationale: Pit urgency should usually increase as tyre-age pressure, service-window overage, and finish infeasibility worsen, while immediately-post-pit freshness should suppress it. A small monotone-constrained LightGBM expert can regularize these directional signals and reduce race-specific memorization that unconstrained trees may exploit.
implementation_hint: Keep the current engineered state features, but train a separate LightGBM with monotone constraints only on obviously directional variables such as `Service_Window_Pressure`, `Service_Age_Distance`, `Finish_Stop_Pressure`, `Cannot_Finish_On_Current_Tyre`, `TyreAge_At_Finish_Est`, and a negative constraint on `Laps_Since_Last_Pit` freshness-style features if present. Blend its OOF ranks with the unconstrained main model.
expected_effect: Small CV gain or flatter fold variance, with better leaderboard robustness if the current model is overfitting race-specific heuristics.
risk: Wrong constraint signs or over-constraining non-monotone interactions can lower AUC.
